content: good enough's not good enough
good enough is good enough
done is better than perfect
Do consequences
No worry before its time
Success = getting what I want.
Magic question: Is there a better way?
Write it down.Write it down. The bluntest pencil is sharper than the sharpest memory.
Write your top three priorities before opening any app.
Read for twenty minutes before checking the news.
Name the feeling you are sitting with before reacting.
Automate transfers to savings on the day you are paid.
Speak about absent people the way you would if they were present.
Take a ten minute walk after every long meeting.
Send a short message to a friend you have not seen this month.
Define what done looks like before you start the task.
Teach the idea to someone else within a day of learning it.
Pause for three slow breaths before replying to a hard message.
Track every expense for one week to see where money actually goes.
Apologise specifically for what you did and not for how it landed.
Drink a full glass of water before your first coffee.
Listen for one full minute longer than feels comfortable.
Break any project into a first step that takes less than five minutes.
Write down the question before you start searching for the answer.
Notice the story you are telling yourself and ask if it is the only one.
Wait twenty four hours before any unplanned purchase over a set amount.
Keep small promises so the big ones become believable.
Stretch for two minutes every time you stand up.
Remember names by repeating them in your first reply.
Finish one task fully before opening the next tab.
Keep a notebook for ideas so your head can be empty for thinking.
Move your body when feelings get stuck in your head.
Review one bill each month and ask whether you still need it.
When you do not know what to say try the truth in fewer words.
Stop eating when you are eighty percent full rather than empty plate.
Make eye contact at the start and end of every conversation.
Set a timer for twenty five minutes and protect that block.
Sleep on hard decisions whenever the clock allows it.
Feelings pass faster when you let them through than when you fight them.
Pay yourself first and let the rest of life fit around what is left.
Praise people behind their back as readily as to their face.
Get sunlight on your face within an hour of waking.
Ask one open question for every statement you make in a chat.
Track streaks rather than perfection so a missed day does not end you.
Read one book outside your field for every five inside it.
Name three things you can see when anxiety starts to rise.
Keep a small fund for genuine emergencies so daily money can flow.
Tip generously when no one is counting.
Lift something heavier than yesterday once a week.
Default to in person for any conversation you would not write down.
Write tomorrow's first task at the end of today.
Doubt your first reading of a strong opinion.
Walk outside when a meeting leaves you wound up.
Choose the cheaper option that you will still use.
Refuse work that requires you to be someone you would not respect.
Sit up tall whenever you notice yourself slumping.
Introduce two people who would benefit from knowing each other.
Plan the week on Sunday so Monday does not start with deciding.
Ask better questions instead of louder ones.
Forgive yourself for the version of you that did not know better.
Buy quality once for things you use every day.
Tell the small truth that no one will ever check.
Treat sleep as the foundation that everything else stands on.
Show up early so the host can breathe.
Aim for one finished thing a day rather than ten started ones.
Read sources you disagree with carefully before dismissing them.
Cry when you need to and let it be a release not a verdict.
Invest in things you understand and skip the ones you do not.
Return what you borrowed in better shape if you can.
Eat slowly enough to taste the third bite.
Compliment the work and not just the person.
Protect mornings for the work that scares you a little.
Sleep is part of thinking so treat it like it is.
Notice when small things annoy you because that is usually a bigger thing in disguise.
Decide what enough looks like before chasing more.
Be the same person whether or not the cameras are running.
Climb stairs whenever they are available and the lift is not faster.
Hold the door even when no one is watching for it.
Cut the to do list in half and finish what is left.
Write to find out what you think.
Take the long way home when the day was long.
Compare prices on the things you buy weekly not the ones you buy yearly.
Keep secrets that are not yours to share.
Walk for ten minutes after every meal you can.
Remember birthdays for the people who never remind you of theirs.
Rest is part of the project not the reward for finishing it.
Steel man the argument you want to attack.
Sit with disappointment before you try to fix it.
Read the small print on anything that recurs.
Apologise to the person you wronged not the audience that watched.
Stop staring at screens for ten minutes every hour.
Bring food when you visit and do not make a thing of it.
Note three wins at the end of each day no matter how small.
Read slowly when the writer is trying to change your mind.
Trust silence more than a quick answer when feelings are loud.
Lend money only when you can afford to call it a gift.
Do the small honest thing even when it costs you a little.
Stand up every thirty minutes while you work.
Listen to understand before you listen to reply.
Finish each work block by writing the next first step.
Read the original source before the summary of it.
Let yourself be bad at something new for longer than feels comfortable.
Round purchases up and let the change build a buffer.
Notice the people who do quiet work and thank them out loud.
Move first then think because the brain follows the body.
Show up when you said you would especially when it is inconvenient.
Choose one thing to be brilliant at this quarter and let others be ordinary.
Spend a minute a day on a question you cannot answer yet.
